name: PMAY (Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana - Housing for All)
benefits: Financial assistance of Rs. 1.2 Lakh in plains and Rs. 1.3 Lakh in hilly/difficult areas for constructing a permanent (pucca) house. It also offers interest subsidies on home loans for low and middle-income groups in urban areas.
eligibility: Families who do not own a pucca house anywhere in India. Families belonging to Economically Weaker Section (EWS, income up to Rs. 3 Lakh), Low Income Group (LIG, income up to Rs. 6 Lakh), and Middle Income Groups (MIG-I & MIG-II, income up to Rs. 18 Lakh) are eligible. Priority is given to women, SC/ST, and disabled individuals.
requiredDocuments: Proof of Land Ownership or Land Lease Agreement
Affidavit stating that no family member owns a pucca house in India

faq.question: What is the financial aid amount?
faq.answer: For rural beneficiaries, PMAY-G provides direct financial aid of Rs. 1.2 Lakh (plains) or Rs. 1.3 Lakh (hilly areas) directly into their bank accounts.

faq.question: Can I apply if I own a kutcha house?
faq.answer: Yes, if you own a kutcha house or a temporary shelter, you are eligible to apply for assistance to build a permanent pucca house.
